front: The results **defy** a simple explanation based on market trends.
back: defy v. 违抗；蔑视；使难以解释
oxford_definition: to refuse to obey or show respect for somebody
collins_definition: to resist or refuse to obey
example_sentences: The findings defy conventional wisdom.
She defied the order and continued the project.
The pattern defies easy explanation.
example_sentences_zh: 这些发现与传统看法不符。
她违抗命令，继续了项目。
这种模式难以简单解释。
example_sources: 自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings_examples: The climber defied the danger of the storm.

front: The new instrument allows scientists to **telescope** distant events in greater detail.
back: telescope n. 望远镜；v. 缩短；压缩
oxford_definition: an instrument used to make distant objects appear nearer and larger
oxford_definition_zh: 使远处物体看起来更近更大的仪器。
collins_definition: to reduce the time or space occupied by something
collins_definition_zh: 缩短某事所占用的时间或空间。
collocations: telescope the schedule 压缩日程
example_sentences: The telescope revealed a distant galaxy.
The company telescoped the review process.
Astronomers use telescopes to observe planets.
example_sentences_zh: 望远镜显示出一个遥远的星系。
公司压缩了审查流程。
天文学家用望远镜观察行星。
example_sources: 自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings: v. 把多个阶段或事件压缩到更短时间内
other_meanings_examples: The documentary telescopes ten years of research into one hour.

front: The firm plans to **deploy** new software across all regional offices.
back: deploy v. 部署；调动；有效利用
oxford_definition: to move soldiers or weapons into a position where they can be used
oxford_definition_zh: 把士兵或武器调到可使用的位置。
collins_definition: to use something effectively or arrange it for a purpose
collins_definition_zh: 有效使用某物或为某目的安排某物。
example_sentences: The company deployed a new payment system.
The agency deployed staff to the affected areas.
Managers must deploy resources carefully.
example_sentences_zh: 公司部署了新的支付系统。
该机构向受影响地区调派了工作人员。
管理者必须谨慎调配资源。
example_sources: 自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings: v. 使某策略或资源发挥作用
other_meanings_examples: The campaign deployed social media to reach younger voters.

front: Regular **meditation** can help employees manage workplace stress.
back: meditation n. 冥想；沉思；思考
oxford_definition: the practice of spending time in silence to improve attention or relaxation
oxford_definition_zh: 通过静默提高注意力或放松身心的练习。
collins_definition: serious thought or study of something
collins_definition_zh: 对某事的认真思考或研究。
example_sentences: Meditation may reduce anxiety.
The retreat offers guided meditation.
He spent the evening in meditation on the decision.
example_sentences_zh: 冥想可能减轻焦虑。
静修活动提供引导式冥想。
他整晚都在思考这个决定。
example_sources: 自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings: n. 对宗教或哲学问题的深思
other_meanings_examples: The essay is a meditation on memory and identity.

front: The company developed a **synthetic** material that is lighter than steel.
back: synthetic adj. 人造的；合成的；虚假的
oxford_definition: made by combining chemical substances rather than produced naturally
oxford_definition_zh: 通过化学物质结合制成而非自然产生的。
collins_definition: made by chemical processes rather than occurring naturally
collins_definition_zh: 通过化学过程制造而非自然形成的。
example_sentences: Synthetic fibers are widely used in clothing.
The model was trained on synthetic data.
The report offered a synthetic view of the evidence.
example_sentences_zh: 合成纤维广泛用于服装。
该模型使用合成数据训练。
这份报告综合呈现了证据。
example_sources: 自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings: adj. 由多个部分综合而成的
other_meanings_examples: The course provides a synthetic account of modern economics.

front: His **compulsive** checking of emails reduced his productivity.
back: compulsive adj. 强迫性的；无法抑制的
oxford_definition: caused by an irresistible desire to do or have something
oxford_definition_zh: 由无法抗拒的欲望导致的。
collins_definition: caused by an irresistible urge to behave in a particular way
collins_definition_zh: 由无法抗拒的冲动导致以某种方式行事的。
collocations: compulsive buyer 强迫性购物者
example_sentences: Compulsive buying can create serious debt.
The device encourages compulsive use.
She has a compulsive need to organize everything.
example_sentences_zh: 强迫性购物可能造成严重债务。
这款设备会诱发无法抑制的使用行为。
她有把一切都整理好的强迫性需求。
example_sources: 自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings: adj. 由心理冲动驱动而难以控制的
other_meanings_examples: Compulsive gambling may damage family relationships.

front: Workers received compensation for injuries sustained at work.
back: compensation n. 补偿；赔偿；薪酬
oxford_definition: something, especially money, given to compensate for loss or injury
oxford_definition_zh: 为弥补损失或伤害而给予的东西，尤其是金钱。
collins_definition: money paid to someone because they have suffered loss or injury
collins_definition_zh: 因遭受损失或伤害而支付给某人的钱。
collocations: compensation package 薪酬福利方案
example_sentences: The airline offered compensation for the delay.
Her salary includes performance compensation.
No compensation can fully replace a lost life.
example_sentences_zh: 航空公司为延误提供了赔偿。
她的薪酬包括绩效奖金。
任何赔偿都无法完全弥补失去的生命。
example_sources: 自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings: n. 对缺陷或不足的弥补
other_meanings_examples: The new feature is compensation for the system's limited storage.

front: The researcher **dedicated** her career to improving public health.
back: dedicate v. 奉献；致力于；题献
oxford_definition: to give a lot of your time and effort to a particular activity
oxford_definition_zh: 把大量时间和精力投入某项活动。
collins_definition: to devote yourself or your time to a particular activity
collins_definition_zh: 把自己或时间投入某项活动。
collocations: dedicate a book to 把书题献给
example_sentences: She dedicated herself to community service.
The scientist dedicated years to the study.
He dedicated the book to his parents.
example_sentences_zh: 她致力于社区服务。
这位科学家多年致力于这项研究。
他把这本书题献给父母。
example_sources: 自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings: v. 在作品或仪式中正式献给某人或某事
other_meanings_examples: The memorial was dedicated to the victims.

front: The policy aims to **stem** the rise in healthcare costs.
back: stem v. 遏制；阻止；n. 茎；根源
oxford_definition: to stop or reduce the growth of something
oxford_definition_zh: 阻止或减少某事物的增长。
collins_definition: to stop something undesirable from spreading or increasing
collins_definition_zh: 阻止不利事物扩散或增加。
collocations: stem the tide 遏制趋势
example_sentences: The reforms were designed to stem corruption.
The plant has a thick central stem.
Most complaints stem from poor communication.
example_sentences_zh: 改革旨在遏制腐败。
这株植物有粗壮的主茎。
大多数投诉源于沟通不良。
example_sources: 自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings: v. stem from 表示起源于
other_meanings_examples: The conflict stems from a misunderstanding.

front: The tax incentive may **spur** investment in renewable energy.
back: spur v. 刺激；促进；n. 激励；马刺
oxford_definition: to encourage somebody to do something
collins_definition: to encourage someone to do something more quickly or enthusiastically
collins_definition_zh: 鼓励某人更快或更积极地做某事。
collocations: on the spur of the moment 一时冲动
example_sentences: Lower taxes could spur economic growth.
The award spurred her to work harder.
He acted on the spur of the moment.
example_sentences_zh: 减税可能刺激经济增长。
这个奖项促使她更加努力。
他一时冲动采取了行动。
example_sources: 自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings: n. 促使行动的动机或刺激因素
other_meanings_examples: The crisis provided a spur to reform.

front: The company introduced a **hybrid** model combining office and remote work.
back: hybrid n. 混合物；杂交品种；adj. 混合的
definition_zh: 混合物；杂交品种；混合的
oxford_definition: a plant or animal produced from two different species
oxford_definition_zh: 由两个不同物种产生的植物或动物。
collins_definition: a thing made by combining two different elements
collins_definition_zh: 由两种不同元素结合而成的事物。
example_sentences: The hybrid vehicle uses both fuel and electricity.
The school adopted a hybrid learning model.
The plant is a hybrid of two varieties.
example_sentences_zh: 这辆混合动力车同时使用燃料和电力。
学校采用了混合式学习模式。
这种植物是两个品种的杂交品种。
example_sources: 自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings: adj. 结合两种不同方法、系统或特征的
other_meanings_examples: The hybrid approach balances efficiency with flexibility.

back: paralysis n. 瘫痪；停滞；无能为力
oxford_definition: a medical condition in which a person is unable to move part or all of the body
oxford_definition_zh: 身体部分或全部无法活动的医学状况。
collins_definition: a state in which a person or organization is unable to act
collins_definition_zh: 个人或组织无法采取行动的状态。
example_sentences: The crisis caused administrative paralysis.
The disease left him with partial paralysis.
Fear can lead to paralysis in decision-making.
example_sentences_zh: 危机导致行政瘫痪。
这种疾病使他部分瘫痪。
恐惧可能导致决策瘫痪。
example_sources: 自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings: n. 因恐惧或犹豫而无法行动
other_meanings_examples: A lack of information produced paralysis among investors.

front: The company targets a younger **segment** of the market.
back: segment n. 部分；群体；市场细分；v. 分割
definition_zh: 部分；群体；市场细分；分割
oxford_definition: a part of something that is separate from the rest
oxford_definition_zh: 与其余部分分开的某一部分。
collins_definition: one of the parts into which something can be divided
collins_definition_zh: 某物可以被分成的部分之一。
collocations: segment of society 社会群体
example_sentences: The report examines a growing market segment.
The road passes through a rural segment of the region.
The audience was segmented by age.
example_sentences_zh: 报告研究了一个不断增长的市场细分。
这条路经过该地区的农村部分。
受众按年龄进行了细分。
example_sources: 自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings: n. 语音或文字中的一段
other_meanings_examples: The final segment of the report summarizes the findings.

front: A senior manager will **supervise** the implementation of the plan.
back: supervise v. 监督；管理；指导
oxford_definition: to be in charge of somebody or something and make sure that work is done correctly
oxford_definition_zh: 负责某人或某事并确保工作正确完成。
collins_definition: to watch over an activity or a person to make sure that everything is done properly
collins_definition_zh: 监督活动或人员以确保一切正确完成。
collocations: supervise a project 监督项目
example_sentences: She supervises a team of engineers.
The inspector supervised the construction work.
Students worked under the supervision of a professor.
example_sentences_zh: 她管理一个工程师团队。
检查员监督了施工工作。
学生在教授指导下工作。
example_sources: 自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings: v. 对学习、培训或研究进行指导
other_meanings_examples: The professor supervises several graduate students.

front: The building was designed to **withstand** severe storms.
back: withstand v. 承受；抵挡；经受住
oxford_definition: to be strong enough not to be harmed or damaged by something
oxford_definition_zh: 足够坚固，不会被某事伤害或损坏。
collins_definition: to resist and remain undamaged by something
collins_definition_zh: 抵抗某事并保持不受损。
example_sentences: The bridge can withstand strong winds.
The policy withstood criticism from experts.
These materials withstand high temperatures.
example_sentences_zh: 这座桥能抵挡强风。
这项政策经受住了专家的批评。
这些材料耐高温。
example_sources: 自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings: v. 经受住长期困难或压力
other_meanings_examples: The small business withstood several years of economic uncertainty.

front: The theatre invited a touring dance **troupe** to perform.
back: troupe n. 剧团；演出团体
oxford_definition: a group of actors, singers or dancers who work together
oxford_definition_zh: 一起工作的演员、歌手或舞者团体。
collins_definition: a group of actors, singers or dancers who perform together
collins_definition_zh: 共同演出的演员、歌手或舞者团体。
example_sentences: The troupe performed in five cities.
A young troupe revived the traditional play.
The circus troupe attracted families.
example_sentences_zh: 这个剧团在五座城市演出。
一个年轻剧团重新演出了这部传统戏剧。
马戏团吸引了许多家庭。

other_meanings: n. 一起从事表演或巡演的一群人
other_meanings_examples: The comedy troupe produces a new show each year.

front: Small businesses can **thrive** when local communities support them.
back: thrive v. 繁荣；茁壮成长；兴旺
oxford_definition: to become and continue to be successful, strong and healthy
oxford_definition_zh: 变得并持续保持成功、强大和健康。
collins_definition: to grow or develop well and successfully
collins_definition_zh: 良好且成功地成长或发展。
collocations: thrive on pressure 在压力下成长
example_sentences: Plants thrive in warm, humid conditions.
The local economy began to thrive.
Some people thrive on creative challenges.
example_sentences_zh: 植物在温暖潮湿的环境中茁壮成长。
当地经济开始繁荣。
有些人在创造性挑战中成长。
example_sources: 自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings: v. 在某种环境或条件下表现得特别好
other_meanings_examples: The organization thrives on cooperation between departments.

front: The campaign aims to **enlist** public support for recycling.
back: enlist v. 谋求支持；征募；参加
oxford_definition: to ask for and get someone's help or support
oxford_definition_zh: 请求并获得某人的帮助或支持。
collins_definition: to persuade someone to help or support you
collins_definition_zh: 说服某人帮助或支持你。
collocations: enlist in the army 入伍
example_sentences: The charity enlisted volunteers for the project.
The leader enlisted public support for the reform.
He enlisted in the army after graduation.
example_sentences_zh: 慈善机构为项目招募了志愿者。
领导人争取公众支持这项改革。
他毕业后入伍。

自拟·考研英语二阶段匹配例句
other_meanings: v. 使某人参与某项行动或事业
other_meanings_examples: The organization enlisted local businesses in its campaign.
